base_architecture.architectural_implication: Integration archetype drives contract-first design centered on CBP and MCP tool definitions. Real-time requires WebSocket + Redis Pub/Sub. Regulatory demands compliance-by-architecture. Event-driven shapes collaborative sessions and cascade notifications.

activated_overlays.name: authentication
activated_overlays.activation_condition: FR-AUTH-001 (LTI 1.3 SSO), FR-AUTH-003 (OAuth2 for Spotify), FR-CHAT-008 (platform auth)
activated_overlays.justification: K-12 SSO via LTI is mandatory for district adoption. OAuth2 for external app auth (Spotify). Platform JWT for session management.

technology_choice.rationale: ltijs handles LTI 1.3 launch, role extraction, grade passback. JWT for session tokens with role + tenant_id. OAuth2 popup for Spotify.

compliance_rules.rule: JWT must include tenant_id (district_id) for RLS context
Session timeout after 8 hours (school day)
OAuth tokens encrypted at rest

banned_patterns.reason: All auth via SSO/LTI. No password management.
COPPA/FERPA session timeout requirement
Use popup pattern — redirect breaks sandbox isolation

activated_overlays.name: authorization
activated_overlays.justification: Teacher authority is core product principle. Students cannot access Mission Control. Teachers cannot suspend district-wide. Admins scoped to own district.

technology_choice.technology: RBAC with JWT claims + Fastify decorators/hooks
technology_choice.rationale: Three fixed roles with well-defined permission boundaries. Fastify preHandler hooks validate role per route.

compliance_rules.rule: Every API endpoint must validate caller role
District admins scoped to own district via RLS

banned_patterns.pattern: Role check in business logic instead of middleware
banned_patterns.reason: Inconsistent enforcement. Role checks must be in route-level hooks.
Type safety. Use TypeScript enum or Zod literal union.

activated_overlays.name: multi_tenant_isolation
activated_overlays.justification: 10K+ districts. FERPA requires zero cross-district data leakage. Database-level enforcement prevents application bugs from leaking data.

technology_choice.technology: PostgreSQL 16 Row-Level Security with SET LOCAL per-transaction via Prisma middleware
technology_choice.rationale: SET LOCAL (not SET) prevents connection pool contamination. Prisma $executeRaw in transaction middleware.

compliance_rules.rule: SET LOCAL app.tenant_id per transaction, not per session
App table is RLS-exempt (platform-global). DistrictAppCatalog is RLS-protected.
Every tenant-scoped table has RLS policy

banned_patterns.reason: Connection pool reuse causes cross-tenant data leakage

banned_patterns.pattern: Application-layer WHERE clause as sole isolation mechanism
banned_patterns.reason: Single bug = FERPA violation. Database enforcement is mandatory.

activated_overlays.name: content_safety_pipeline
activated_overlays.justification: K-12 platform serving children. 4-stage pipeline: PII → injection → LLM classification → crisis detection. Every message crossing trust boundaries must be classified.

technology_choice.technology: Regex (PII, injection) + Claude Sonnet (classification) + keyword scan (crisis)
technology_choice.rationale: Sequential pipeline: PII (<50ms) → injection (<20ms) → LLM (<500ms) → crisis (<10ms). Total <600ms.

compliance_rules.rule: Crisis detection ALWAYS runs regardless of prior stage verdicts
PII stripped before message reaches LLM
All safety events logged to immutable audit trail

banned_patterns.reason: Insufficient for K-12. Multi-layer defense required.
All inputs are untrusted. No exceptions.

banned_patterns.pattern: Blocking crisis detection based on prior stage results
banned_patterns.reason: Crisis must always be detected — cannot be masked by earlier block.

activated_overlays.name: real_time_streaming
activated_overlays.activation_condition: NFR-PERF-004 (<100ms WebSocket relay), FR-CTRL-005 (Mission Control), FR-COLLAB-003 (collaborative sync)
activated_overlays.justification: Mission Control needs real-time student monitoring. Collaborative sessions need sub-200ms state sync. CBP command relay needs WebSocket delivery.

technology_choice.rationale: Multi-worker Node.js needs cross-worker broadcast. Redis Pub/Sub channels per classroom keep broadcast targeted.

compliance_rules.rule: WebSocket auth via query param JWT (browsers can't set headers on WS)
Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff on client disconnect

banned_patterns.reason: Latency and resource waste. WebSocket required.
Doesn't scale across workers. Must use Redis Pub/Sub.

activated_overlays.name: iframe_sandboxing
activated_overlays.activation_condition: FR-APP-003 (iframe rendering), FR-APP-007 (resource limits), FR-STATE-005 (origin validation)
activated_overlays.justification: Third-party app isolation for child safety. Browser-native sandboxing prevents DOM access, navigation, form submission.

technology_choice.technology: iframe sandbox attributes + CSP frame-ancestors + postMessage origin validation + 64KB message limit
technology_choice.rationale: Browser-native isolation. No custom runtime needed. Well-understood security model.

compliance_rules.rule: sandbox='allow-scripts' only — no allow-same-origin unless explicitly approved
All postMessage origins validated against allowlist before processing
Message size checked before JSON parse (64KB limit)

banned_patterns.reason: Allows iframe to access parent DOM and cookies
Allows message injection from any origin
All tool invocations must route through backend (FR-STATE-004)

activated_overlays.name: observability
activated_overlays.justification: AI-native Level 3 product. 100% LLM trace coverage required. Self-hosted Langfuse for COPPA compliance.

technology_choice.technology: Langfuse self-hosted (Docker on Railway) + structured logging (pino via Fastify)
technology_choice.rationale: Langfuse self-hosted ensures US data residency. Pino is Fastify's default logger. Pseudonymous user IDs — no PII in traces.

compliance_rules.rule: No PII in Langfuse traces — pseudonymous user_id only
Never block user-facing requests on observability failures
All LLM calls traced with parent-child span structure

banned_patterns.reason: COPPA/FERPA violation

banned_patterns.pattern: Synchronous Langfuse calls in request path
banned_patterns.reason: Observability failure would block users

banned_patterns.pattern: Cloud-hosted observability without US data residency
banned_patterns.reason: COPPA data residency requirement

activated_overlays.name: rate_limiting
activated_overlays.justification: Prevent abuse, protect LLM costs, enforce fair usage per role.

technology_choice.rationale: Redis provides distributed rate limiting across workers. Fastify plugin handles per-route configuration.

activated_overlays.name: audit_logging
activated_overlays.activation_condition: FR-COMP-003 (FERPA audit trails), FR-OBS-003 (safety event audit)
activated_overlays.justification: FERPA requires audit trails for all access to student records. COPPA requires safety event tracking.

technology_choice.technology: Immutable audit_events table in PostgreSQL + Fastify onResponse hook
technology_choice.rationale: Append-only table for compliance. Fastify hook captures every API call with user_id, action, resource, timestamp.

compliance_rules.rule: Audit records are append-only — no updates or deletes
Every API call logged with: who, what, when, tenant_id

activated_overlays.name: encryption
activated_overlays.activation_condition: FR-COMP-004 (US data residency), COPPA/FERPA data protection
activated_overlays.justification: Student data must be encrypted in transit and at rest.

technology_choice.technology: TLS 1.3 (in transit) + PostgreSQL encryption at rest (Railway managed) + AES-256 for OAuth tokens
technology_choice.rationale: Railway manages TLS termination and disk encryption. Application-level encryption for sensitive tokens.

activated_overlays.name: health_checks
activated_overlays.activation_condition: NFR-REL-001 (99% uptime), L-002 (health must report capability status)
activated_overlays.justification: Health checks must report product capability, not just infrastructure connectivity. If chess app is degraded, status = degraded.

technology_choice.technology: Fastify health check route + capability probes for each subsystem
technology_choice.rationale: Per L-002: health endpoint tests actual functionality. Probes for DB, Redis, Anthropic API, each registered app.

compliance_rules.rule: Health check must distinguish infrastructure vs product capability status
If any critical capability is down, overall status = degraded, not healthy

activated_overlays.name: graceful_degradation
activated_overlays.activation_condition: FR-INFRA-001 (Redis failure), FR-INFRA-002 (PostgreSQL failure), FR-INFRA-003 (WebSocket failure), FR-CHAT-007 (app failure)
activated_overlays.justification: Every external dependency has a defined failure mode and fallback behavior.

technology_choice.technology: Circuit breaker pattern + fallback behaviors per component
technology_choice.rationale: Each subsystem has defined degradation: Redis down → disable real-time, keep chat. App down → collapse card, AI acknowledges.

activated_overlays.name: workflow_state_machine
activated_overlays.activation_condition: FR-APP-004 (app lifecycle FSM), FR-AUTH-004 (COPPA consent flow), FR-SAFE-007 (app review pipeline)
activated_overlays.justification: Multiple lifecycle state machines require formal transition rules, guards, and invalid transition handling.

technology_choice.technology: XState or custom TypeScript state machine with Zod-validated transitions
technology_choice.rationale: XState provides formal state machine with guards and actions. Alternative: lightweight custom implementation with Zod for transition validation.

activated_overlays.name: secrets_management
activated_overlays.activation_condition: Multiple API keys (Anthropic, Spotify, OpenWeatherMap, LTI, Langfuse, JWT secret)
activated_overlays.justification: Sensitive credentials must be externalized and never committed to code.

technology_choice.technology: Railway environment variables + .env files for local dev
technology_choice.rationale: Railway natively supports environment variables per service. No need for external secrets manager at V1 scale.

banned_patterns.pattern: API keys in source code or config files committed to git
banned_patterns.reason: Security 101
Dev/staging/production must use separate credentials
